name: Cinematic Camera
description: Smoothly animate the camera through a sequence of positions and look targets for cutscenes and cinematic sequences. Supports smooth interpolation, wait pauses between steps, and looping.
documentation:
## Cinematic Camera
Animate the camera through waypoints for cutscenes.

### Attributes
- **Steps** - JSON array of camera waypoints (see format below)
- **Auto Play** - Start automatically when the game begins
- **Loop** - Restart from the beginning after completing all steps

### Step Format
```json
[
  { "to": {"x":0,"y":5,"z":10}, "lookAt": {"x":0,"y":0,"z":0}, "duration": 3, "wait": 1 },
  { "from": {"x":0,"y":5,"z":10}, "to": {"x":10,"y":2,"z":0}, "duration": 2 }
]
```

### Events
- **play** - Start the cinematic sequence
- **stop** - Stop and freeze at current position

### Tips
- Use `from` to override the starting position of a step (defaults to current camera position)
- Use `lookAtFrom`/`lookAtTo` for animated look targets
- Combine with Trigger behavior for interactive cutscenes
author: stemstudio

steps.name: Steps
steps.description: JSON array of cinematic steps. Each step has: to (required), from, lookAt, lookAtFrom, lookAtTo, duration (seconds), wait (seconds).

autoPlay.name: Auto Play
autoPlay.description: Start the cinematic sequence automatically when the game begins.

loop.name: Loop
loop.description: Loop the sequence from the beginning after all steps complete.
